title: Weak signal — first thing
steps: Place router higher.
Move away from cabinet, TV and metal.
Test signal in problem area.
warnings: The app does not change router settings automatically.
dontDo: do not hide router in cabinet
do not buy gear first

title: Weak signal — full plan
summary: Improve range with router placement and test map.
steps: Test speed near router.
Test in problem area.
Move router higher and more central.
Avoid cabinets, floor, metal and TV equipment.
Test 3–5 points at home.
If many walls block signal, consider mesh.
warnings: Do not reset router without saving access details.
dontDo: do not place router behind TV
do not buy repeater before placement test

title: Slow speed — compare near and far
summary: Check whether the problem is internet or WiFi.
steps: Test near router.
Test in problem room.
Compare speed and ping.
warnings: WiFi result depends on distance, walls and device.
dontDo: do not judge connection from one room only

title: Slow speed — checklist
summary: Separate operator, WiFi and device load issues.
steps: Test near router.
Compare wired test if possible.
Test several rooms.
Pause big downloads and streaming.
Check 2.4 GHz vs 5 GHz.
If near router is good and far bad, improve range or mesh.
warnings: The app does not log into router automatically.
dontDo: do not change many settings at once
do not reset router first

title: Disconnects — first test
summary: Check if one device disconnects or the whole network.
steps: Compare two devices.
Check near router.
Restart router normally.
warnings: Do not factory reset as first step.
dontDo: do not factory reset without operator details

title: Disconnects — stability plan
summary: Find out whether the cause is device, range, load or router.
steps: Write time and place of disconnect.
Test another device in the same place.
Test near router.
Check if issue worsens with many devices.
Restart router normally and wait.
If whole network drops, check provider or router service.
warnings: Do not change channels, modes and security all at once.
dontDo: do not factory reset as first-level diagnosis

title: Problem in one room
summary: Usually caused by walls, distance or router position.
steps: Test near router and in that room.
Count walls between router and room.
Move router higher or closer to home center.
dontDo: do not buy extender without location test

title: One-room problem — plan
summary: Test map and decision: placement, mesh or access point.
steps: Test near router, halfway and in problem room.
Write wall count and material.
Move router higher and more central.
If signal dies after many walls, consider mesh.
If Ethernet reaches the room, use access point.
Repeat the same tests after changes.
warnings: A repeater placed in a dead zone repeats weak signal.
dontDo: do not place repeater where signal is already poor

title: Many devices — check load
summary: Many devices can overload network or router.
steps: Count active devices.
Pause streaming or downloads.
Check if problem affects all devices.
dontDo: do not assume range is the only issue

title: Many devices — full checklist
steps: Group devices: work, streaming, smart home, consoles.
Check if issue happens during big downloads.
Move important devices closer or to 5 GHz when range is good.
Keep many smart home devices on 2.4 GHz.
If router is old, consider newer router or mesh.
Test one change at a time.
warnings: The app does not switch bands or log into router automatically.
dontDo: do not rename networks without preparing smart home devices

title: Router in bad place
summary: Cabinet, floor or behind TV can strongly reduce WiFi.
steps: Take router out of cabinet.
Place it higher.
Move away from metal and electronics.
dontDo: do not cover router
do not place it on floor

title: Bad router place — plan
summary: Improve router position before buying extra hardware.
steps: Test current place.
Move router to open shelf.
Place higher than floor and not behind TV.
Move away from metal, aquarium, microwave and thick walls.
Repeat same point tests.
If improvement is small, consider mesh or access point.
warnings: Do not force or bend fiber cable.
dontDo: do not bend fiber
do not hide router in closed cabinet

title: Many walls — consider mesh
summary: One router may not be enough behind many walls.
steps: Count walls to problem area.
Test halfway point.
Consider mesh or access point.
dontDo: do not expect full speed behind many walls

title: Many walls — expansion plan
summary: When mesh, repeater or access point makes sense.
steps: Test near router, halfway and in problem area.
If halfway has good signal, mesh can help.
If Ethernet exists, access point is more stable.
Place repeater where signal is good, not in dead zone.
For multi-floor home consider one node per floor.
After purchase repeat same tests.
warnings: Mesh will not fix slow provider connection if issue already exists near router.
dontDo: do not buy extender without checking halfway point

title: Basic WiFi diagnosis
summary: Compare result near router and in the problem place.
steps: Test near router.
Test in problem place.
Check router position.
warnings: The app does not change router settings automatically.
dontDo: do not change settings without notes

title: Universal WiFi plan
summary: Safe order for improving home WiFi.
steps: Measure near router first.
Measure 3–5 points at home.
Improve router place: higher, central, not in cabinet.
Check walls and device count.
Use 5 GHz for close fast devices and 2.4 GHz for farther or IoT devices.
If walls remain the issue, consider mesh or access point.
warnings: This is a self-check checklist, not automatic router configuration.
dontDo: do not reset router without saved data
do not change many settings at once
